title: Matt Reeves Puts Humanity in 'The Planet of the Apes' | The Big Picture (Ep. 18)
format: Chris Ryan on how directors get vaulted into franchise jobs, then Matt Reeves on the mocap grind behind War for the Planet of the Apes and his coming Batman.

films.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming
films.note: John Watts jumps from two tiny films to the A-list; Ryan reads him as a Russo-type the Marvel people like.

films.title: Cop Car
films.note: One of Watts's two pre-Marvel movies. Ryan notes he directs kids well.

films.title: Clown
films.note: Watts's small horror film, the other pre-Homecoming credit.

films.title: Baby Driver
films.note: Used as the yardstick: does Watts have a non-franchise swing of his own in him?

films.title: The Book of Henry
films.note: Colin Trevorrow's misfire; Ryan thinks it's unfair to judge his Star Wars off it.

films.title: Transformers: The Last Knight
films.note: Bay goes back to the well; poorly received, soft box office.

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: Nolan's other choice: still big, but an intense, fat-free IMAX ride rather than a Transformers-scale blockbuster.

films.title: Pain & Gain
films.note: Ryan's alt-history: Bay has spent a decade trying to make Nolan movies like this one.

films.title: 13 Hours: The Secret Soldiers of Benghazi
films.note: The other Bay passion project; couldn't find an audience.

segments.heading: Matt Reeves on 'War for the Planet of the Apes'

films.title: War for the Planet of the Apes
films.note: His second Apes film; the first with no scene lacking apes. Shot on Alexa 65, a year of writing with Mark Bomback, over a year in post matching Andy Serkis's face onto Caesar shot by shot.

films.title: Dawn of the Planet of the Apes
films.note: He stepped in after Rupert Wyatt left and made it in two years, not the usual three; a crash course in motion capture he chose War to build on.

films.title: Rise of the Planet of the Apes
films.note: Where Reeves first felt full emotional identification with a CG character.

films.title: Planet of the Apes
films.note: The endpoint the trilogy is written toward; you get the Statue of Liberty reveal only once, so the whole burden shifts from what to how.

films.title: Beneath the Planet of the Apes
films.note: His childhood favorite of the sequels; the mutants unmasking and praying to the bomb freaked him out.

films.title: Cloverfield
films.note: An independent film made at Bad Robot, not the studio picture people assume; Abrams told him the VFX were the easy part.

films.title: Let Me In
films.note: A remake he turned down until it became the most personal thing he could make; John Ajvide Lindqvist told him to do it.

films.title: Let the Right One In
films.note: The Tomas Alfredson original he loved and was reluctant to remake.

films.title: The Pallbearer
films.note: His first film, a Miramax comedy with David Schwimmer and Gwyneth Paltrow; he was after Hal Ashby-style 'painful comedies' and it didn't fully work.

films.title: The Batman
films.note: Next up: a noir, heavily point-of-view take that finds a parallel between Batman and Caesar as tortured men trying to do right in a corrupt world.
